[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Terence_Crawford&action=edit&section=39 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Crawford has two sisters, Latisha and Shawntay.[[285]](#cite_note-292) He has been in a relationship with Alindra Person since the early 2000s. They first met in their hometown of [Omaha, Nebraska](/wiki/Omaha,_Nebraska "Omaha, Nebraska"), and have six children together.[[286]](#cite_note-293) Crawford also has another child with another woman.[[287]](#cite_note-294)

Crawford reportedly boasts a large [real estate](/wiki/Real_estate "Real estate") portfolio consisting of over 100 properties in [Missouri](/wiki/Missouri "Missouri"), [Colorado](/wiki/Colorado "Colorado"), and [Nebraska](/wiki/Nebraska "Nebraska").[[288]](#cite_note-295) As of 2025, he has earned more than $100 million in the ring, brings in at least $2 million annually from brand partnerships, and his real estate holdings are valued at over $20 million.[[289]](#cite_note-296)

During fight camps, he moves to his second house in [Colorado Springs](/wiki/Colorado_Springs,_Colorado "Colorado Springs, Colorado") and trains at Triple Threat Gym.[[290]](#cite_note-297)[[291]](#cite_note-298)

Just three days after defeating [Ricky Burns](/wiki/Ricky_Burns "Ricky Burns") to capture his first world title, Crawford was honored with the [Key to the City of Omaha, Nebraska](/wiki/List_of_Keys_to_the_City_in_the_United_States#Omaha "List of Keys to the City in the United States"). The [Omaha City Council](/wiki/Omaha_City_Council "Omaha City Council") also declared March 4, 2014, as Terence "Bud" Crawford Day.[[292]](#cite_note-:9-299)

In August 2018, Larimore Avenue, where he grew up, was renamed Terence "Bud" Crawford Street.[[293]](#cite_note-300) Crawford still resides in Omaha along with his family, where he is a celebrated figure.

In May 2025, the [University of Nebraska Omaha](/wiki/University_of_Nebraska_Omaha "University of Nebraska Omaha") invited Crawford to be the keynote speaker at the commencement ceremony for graduating students.[[294]](#cite_note-301) After delivering his speech, he was honored with the inaugural Spirit of the Maverick award.[[295]](#cite_note-302)

### Business partnerships

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Terence_Crawford&action=edit&section=40 "Edit section: Business partnerships")]

In May 2017, Crawford signed a deal with sports equipment brand [Everlast](/wiki/Everlast_(brand) "Everlast (brand)").[[296]](#cite_note-303) In April 2019, he partnered with premium [hemp](/wiki/Hemp "Hemp") [CBD](/wiki/Cannabidiol "Cannabidiol") brand Populum, serving as an advocate for the use of CBD among boxers.[[297]](#cite_note-304)

After being sponsored by [Prime Hydration](/wiki/Prime_(drink) "Prime (drink)") for his July fight with [Errol Spence Jr.](/wiki/Errol_Spence_Jr. "Errol Spence Jr."), Crawford formalized a partnership deal with the company in November 2023.[[298]](#cite_note-305)

In 2024, Crawford signed a 10-year licensing agreement for the boxing video game [*Undisputed*](/wiki/Undisputed_(video_game) "Undisputed (video game)"), a deal that includes equity ownership and a share of the franchise.[[299]](#cite_note-306)

In 2025, Crawford entered into a partnership with Crown Coins Casino to serve as its brand ambassador.[[300]](#cite_note-307) As part of the collaboration, Crown Coins sponsored Crawford and launched themed slot games featuring his likeness.[[301]](#cite_note-308)[[302]](#cite_note-309)

### In popular culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Terence_Crawford&action=edit&section=41 "Edit section: In popular culture")]

Crawford was a featured cover athlete on [*The Ring* magazine's](/wiki/The_Ring_(magazine) "The Ring (magazine)") September 2015, January 2019, May 2019, and November 2025 issues.[[303]](#cite_note-310)[[304]](#cite_note-311)[[305]](#cite_note-312)

Crawford was featured in the [*Creed*](/wiki/Rocky_(franchise) "Rocky (franchise)") film franchise, appearing in [Creed III](/wiki/Creed_III "Creed III") in 2023.[[306]](#cite_note-313) In October 2025, *Undisputed* released a "Championship Edition" of its video game featuring Crawford on the cover.[[307]](#cite_note-:16-314) In November 2025, Crawford was featured by [*Haute Living*](/wiki/Haute_Living "Haute Living"), modeling some expensive [Ulysse Nardin](/wiki/Ulysse_Nardin "Ulysse Nardin") watches.[[308]](#cite_note-315)

After winning the [undisputed welterweight championship](/wiki/Undisputed_championship_(boxing) "Undisputed championship (boxing)") in 2023, the city of Omaha held a parade in Crawford's honor, drawing thousands of supporters.[[309]](#cite_note-316) The city held a second parade on September 27, 2025, following his victory over [Canelo Álvarez](/wiki/Canelo_%C3%81lvarez "Canelo Álvarez") to become the undisputed super middleweight champion; the celebration drew roughly 100,000 supporters,[[310]](#cite_note-317) and Mayor [John Ewing Jr.](/wiki/John_Ewing_Jr. "John Ewing Jr.") presented Crawford with another key to the city.[[311]](#cite_note-318)[[312]](#cite_note-:12-319)

In [Google's](/wiki/Google "Google") list of top trending searches for 2025, Crawford ranked as the most-searched athlete of the year.[[313]](#cite_note-320)

#### WWE appearances

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Terence_Crawford&action=edit&section=42 "Edit section: WWE appearances")]

On July 19, 2024, Crawford was ringside for *[WWE SmackDown](/wiki/WWE_SmackDown "WWE SmackDown")* in his hometown of Omaha, Nebraska.[[314]](#cite_note-:18-321) During the event, he assisted [Cody Rhodes](/wiki/Cody_Rhodes "Cody Rhodes") by handing him a chair which he used to hit [Grayson Waller](/wiki/Grayson_Waller "Grayson Waller").[[315]](#cite_note-322)

Crawford appeared on Friday Night SmackDown again the following week, this time stepping into the ring to confront Waller and [Austin Theory](/wiki/Austin_Theory "Austin Theory"). Waller warned Crawford by saying "Look at me when I'm talking to you. If you take one more step, my boy Austin Theory will knock you out." Ignoring his warning, Crawford knocked Theory to the ground with one punch.[[316]](#cite_note-:19-323)[[317]](#cite_note-324)

### Legal issues

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Terence_Crawford&action=edit&section=43 "Edit section: Legal issues")]

In September 2008, after shooting dice with a few friends, Crawford was shot on the right side of his head, right below his ear, while sitting in the driver's seat of his 1986 [Oldsmobile Cutlass Supreme](/wiki/Oldsmobile_Cutlass_Supreme "Oldsmobile Cutlass Supreme"). Reportedly, the nine-millimeter bullet changed trajectory after initially hitting the rear windshield. Had it not, it would have hit more directly, penetrated deeply, and done enough damage to be fatal. Despite the badly bleeding wound, Crawford was still able to drive himself to the hospital, where he received several stitches and was later discharged. Crawford fought and won his fifth match of the year against Michael Williams less than two months after the incident.[[318]](#cite_note-325) When asked if he found out who the shooter was and if he was caught, Crawford responded "The shooter is dead, he got shot in the head".[[319]](#cite_note-326)

In 2016, Crawford faced legal issues stemming from an incident involving his [1984 Chevrolet Monte Carlo](/wiki/Chevrolet_Monte_Carlo#Fourth_generation_(1981–1988) "Chevrolet Monte Carlo") at an Omaha, Nebraska, [auto body shop](/wiki/Automobile_repair_shop "Automobile repair shop"). After paying about half of a $2,500 repainting fee, he disputed the quality and delay of the work; when the shop refused to release the car without full payment, Crawford returned with three friends and removed the vehicle from a hydraulic lift, causing an estimated $5,000 in damages. The shop owner said he felt threatened and alleged Crawford pushed him while leaving. Crawford voluntarily surrendered to police on April 22 and was charged with four [misdemeanors](/wiki/Misdemeanor "Misdemeanor")—[destruction of property](/wiki/Property_damage "Property damage"), [theft of service](/wiki/Theft_of_services "Theft of services"), [misdemeanor assault](/wiki/Assault "Assault"), and [trespassing](/wiki/Trespass_to_land "Trespass to land").[[320]](#cite_note-327) In September, he was convicted of property damage and disorderly conduct and, in December, sentenced to 90 days in jail, two years of probation, and 120 hours of community service.[[321]](#cite_note-328)[[322]](#cite_note-329) The jail sentence was later overturned on appeal, while the probation and community service terms remained in effect.[[323]](#cite_note-330)[[324]](#cite_note-331)

In the early hours of his 38th birthday, just before 1:30 a.m. on September 28, 2025, Crawford was held at gunpoint during a traffic stop in Omaha after officers stopped his sedan for reckless driving and observed a firearm on the driver's side floorboard. Police ordered Crawford and three other occupants out of the vehicle with weapons drawn; all were legally permitted to carry firearms, and Crawford was cited for reckless driving. Omaha mayor [John Ewing Jr.](/wiki/John_Ewing_Jr. "John Ewing Jr.") and Police Chief Todd Schmaderer announced a review of the incident, with Schmaderer authorizing an internal affairs investigation that later found no policy violations.[[325]](#cite_note-332)[[326]](#cite_note-333)[[327]](#cite_note-:15-334) Body-camera footage showed the initial interaction was cordial, with Crawford explaining the new car "got away from him," but an officer who did not hear the disclosure of legal firearms drew his weapon upon seeing the gun, and the occupants were briefly handcuffed before being released. Schmaderer said the video would not be released without Crawford's consent.[[328]](#cite_note-335)[[327]](#cite_note-:15-334)

Just days after announcing his retirement from boxing, Crawford was named in a [civil](/wiki/Civil_law_(common_law) "Civil law (common law)") [lawsuit](/wiki/Lawsuit "Lawsuit") filed December 18, 2025, in [Manhattan Civil Court](/wiki/New_York_City_Civil_Court "New York City Civil Court") by New York jeweler Mazza New York, which is seeking at least $1,500,000 in damages over an alleged failed promotional agreement. The suit claims that during a February 2024 visit, Crawford received a [Patek Philippe](/wiki/Patek_Philippe "Patek Philippe") Aquanaut valued at about $139,000 in exchange for a $35,000 cash payment and promotional placement of the jeweler's logo on his fight trunks.[[329]](#cite_note-336) According to the filing, the logo detached early in Crawford's August 2024 bout against [Israil Madrimov](/wiki/Israil_Madrimov "Israil Madrimov") and, despite assurances it would appear in his next fight, was not displayed during his September bout against [Canelo Álvarez](/wiki/Canelo_%C3%81lvarez "Canelo Álvarez"). Mazza estimates the lost exposure from the Álvarez fight alone at approximately $1,500,000 and is seeking return of the watch—now valued at more than $155,000—along with damages for breach of contract, unjust enrichment, and conversion.[[330]](#cite_note-337)

## Charitable work

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Terence_Crawford&action=edit&section=44 "Edit section: Charitable work")]

### B&B Sports Academy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Terence_Crawford&action=edit&section=45 "Edit section: B&B Sports Academy")]

In October 2013, Crawford co-founded B&B Sports Academy, a nonprofit gym dedicated to building the body, mind and character of young people, including at-risk youth in need of positive, structured activities.[[331]](#cite_note-338) The academy is free for kids and includes a mandatory tutoring program that requires everyone to maintain at least a C average in school.[[332]](#cite_note-339)

### Other charitable work

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Terence_Crawford&action=edit&section=46 "Edit section: Other charitable work")]

In 2013, Crawford started an Anti-Bullying campaign where he went to schools all over [Omaha](/wiki/Omaha,_Nebraska "Omaha, Nebraska") to discuss bullying, how to deal with it, and how to prevent it.[[333]](#cite_note-340)

In 2014, Crawford traveled with his former teacher Jamie Nollette to [Rwanda](/wiki/Rwanda "Rwanda") and [Uganda](/wiki/Uganda "Uganda") on several charitable missions to help build and drill wells to create clean water for communities.[[334]](#cite_note-341)

In September 2023, Crawford participated in a celebrity charity basketball game staged to benefit the Lifedriven Foundation, a non-profit organization committed to supporting adolescents diagnosed with cancer, and their families.[[335]](#cite_note-342)

In 2024, he returned to Uganda for charity and other philanthropic reasons.[[336]](#cite_note-343)

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Terence_Crawford&action=edit&section=67 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-2)** Listed by [BoxRec](/wiki/BoxRec "BoxRec") as southpaw, but regularly [switch-hits](/wiki/Boxing_styles_and_technique#Switch-hitter "Boxing styles and technique") as an [orthodox](/wiki/Orthodox_stance "Orthodox stance").
2. **[^](#cite_ref-4)** Four-belt era: [World Boxing Association](/wiki/World_Boxing_Association "World Boxing Association") (WBA) ([Super version](/wiki/WBA_(Super) "WBA (Super)")), [World Boxing Council](/wiki/World_Boxing_Council "World Boxing Council") (WBC), [International Boxing Federation](/wiki/International_Boxing_Federation "International Boxing Federation") (IBF), and [World Boxing Organization](/wiki/World_Boxing_Organization "World Boxing Organization") (WBO) titles.
3. **[^](#cite_ref-5)** Joining [Henry Armstrong](/wiki/Henry_Armstrong "Henry Armstrong"), who accomplished the feat simultaneously.
4. **[^](#cite_ref-6)** Crawford would later be joined in this achievement by [Naoya Inoue](/wiki/Naoya_Inoue "Naoya Inoue") and [Oleksandr Usyk](/wiki/Oleksandr_Usyk "Oleksandr Usyk").
5. **[^](#cite_ref-11)** Three-belt era: [World Boxing Association](/wiki/World_Boxing_Association "World Boxing Association") (WBA) ([Undisputed version](/wiki/WBA_(Undisputed) "WBA (Undisputed)")), [World Boxing Council](/wiki/World_Boxing_Council "World Boxing Council") (WBC), and [International Boxing Federation](/wiki/International_Boxing_Federation "International Boxing Federation") (IBF) titles.
6. **[^](#cite_ref-17)** Joined [Dingaan Thobela](/wiki/Dingaan_Thobela "Dingaan Thobela").
7. **[^](#cite_ref-22)** Tied with [Naoya Inoue](/wiki/Naoya_Inoue "Naoya Inoue"),[[14]](#cite_note-20) [Roberto Durán](/wiki/Roberto_Dur%C3%A1n "Roberto Durán"), and [Naseem Hamed](/wiki/Naseem_Hamed "Naseem Hamed"); Behind [Gennady Golovkin](/wiki/Gennady_Golovkin "Gennady Golovkin") and [Wilfredo Gómez](/wiki/Wilfredo_G%C3%B3mez "Wilfredo Gómez").[[15]](#cite_note-KO-21)
8. **[^](#cite_ref-344)** First undisputed light welterweight champion of the four-belt era.
9. **[^](#cite_ref-d_345-0)** First and only undisputed welterweight champion of the four-belt era.
10. **[^](#cite_ref-362)** [ESPN](/wiki/ESPN "ESPN") first introduced the list in 2022.
11. **[^](#cite_ref-403)** Selected as one of the eight best fights of 2014.
12. **[^](#cite_ref-418)** Shared with [Naoya Inoue](/wiki/Naoya_Inoue "Naoya Inoue").
